flashcards.definition: Energy moves through ecosystems in one direction: sun → producers → consumers → decomposers; 10% rule between trophic levels.
Carbon, nitrogen, phosphorus, and water cycles — human activities have disrupted all of them.
Maximum population an environment can sustain; influenced by resources, space, disease, and predation.
Toxins concentrate in organisms (bioaccumulation) and increase at each trophic level (biomagnification).
Environmental, economic, and social sustainability must all be addressed for true sustainability.
